cpu: i7-11800H (16T, Tiger Lake, AVX2+AVX-512); CUDA hidden (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=-1, CAMELID_CUDA_RESIDENT_{DECODE,PREFILL}=0)
source_runtime_head: a86fb46b + Phase 2 working-tree change (q4_k_cpu_block_dot default-on; committed in the following commit)

comparator.flags: /completion CPU, -ngl 0 -ctk f32 -ctv f32 -fa off --no-repack; temperature 0, top_k 1, seed 0, cache_prompt false, return_tokens true

camelid.path: CPU K-quant block-dot decode at the matmul chokepoint (matmul_rhs_transposed_q4_k_block_dot / q6_k), gated CAMELID_X86_Q4K_DECODE (now DEFAULT-ON).
camelid.q4_k_kernel: diffusion_gemma::refmath::q4_k_dot_arm -> q4_k_dot_avx2 (AVX2, bit-identical to scalar; engaged since avx2 detected)
camelid.q6_k_kernel: q6_k_wire_row_dot (8-lane f32 scalar oracle; AVX2 sibling NOT yet wired for the 8-lane order — perf follow-up)
camelid.guard_note: In serve CPU mode the f32 weight-materialization budget guard FALSE-POSITIVES on K-quant block-dot models (estimates ~16 GB f32 materialization the wire-only block-dot never does, because binding_runs_on_resident_gpu is false on CPU). Bypassed for this run with CAMELID_MAX_CPU_WEIGHT_MATERIALIZATION_BYTES; bench-generate is unaffected (different load path). Follow-up: treat K-quant linears as non-materializing when q4_k_cpu_block_dot is enabled.

result.methodology: raw-prompt token+text decode parity, 3 confident probes at 1/5/50 tokens, CPU camelid vs CPU llama.cpp
result.confident_probes_token_identical: France (depth 50) + def fibonacci code (depth 50) fully token-identical; Q:2+2 identical to depth 5 then a benign f32 near-tie at depth (coherent output)
result.claim: CPU K-quant block-dot decode of Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M is token-AND-text-identical to llama.cpp acd79d6 on confident probes (incl. code to depth 50); near-tie at depth documented. Same bar as the GPU primary bundle.
result.fixes: Turns the pre-existing CPU K-quant crash (data_len=0, no f32 fallback for wire-only K-quant) into parity-correct output by promoting CAMELID_X86_Q4K_DECODE to default-on. GPU resident lane never reaches this path, so unaffected.

speed.ratio: ~0.55x of llama.cpp CPU — consistent with the known ~0.6x CPU tiled-GEMM gap; CPU decode is DRAM-bandwidth-bound on this box.

followups: Q6_K AVX2 (8-lane order): write an AVX2 kernel bit-identical to q6_k_wire_row_dot's 8-lane f32 reduction (NOT refmath q6_k_dot_avx2, which uses a single-accumulator order and would change the bit pattern), then wire it into q6_k_block_dot_core. Likely bandwidth-bound/null on this box; needs a bit-identical test + measurement.
CPU weight-materialization guard: bypass for K-quant linears when the block-dot is enabled (mirror binding_runs_on_resident_gpu).
Linux/macOS greedy-parity confirmation for the now-default-on CPU K-quant path.
claim_boundary: Exact-row CPU-decode smoke for this Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M GGUF on Windows x86_64 (AVX2/AVX-512). Token-identical on confident probes; near-ties at depth documented. NOT claimed: bit-exact parity on open-ended generation, other models/quants, Linux/macOS parity (follow-up), or a CPU throughput claim.
